page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the legacy N1 family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 26.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 6.5 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for boot and data volumes.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the n1-highmem-4 sits in the lower performance tiers. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests register in the bottom 25%, with scores of 1109.89 and 2914.17 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 162.39 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 25.86 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Geekbench scores further confirm this positioning, with a single-core score of 924.0 and a multi-core score of 2032.0. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported via AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets, though AVX-512 is unavailable.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highmem-4 provides a higher memory-to-vCPU ratio than standard instances, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance compute. However, its older Intel Xeon architecture results in lower performance-per-resource compared to newer generations. This instance is best suited for moderate database operations, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose applications with elevated memory footprints but low CPU utilization.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 26.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. It lacks integrated GPUs and local storage. Benchmark data places this instance in the lower performance tiers, with a single-core Geekbench score of 924.0 and a multi-core score of 2032.0. Memory bandwidth reaches 25.86 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and Redis SET operations perform at 2.75 million operations per second. Due to its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that are not CPU-intensive. The n1-highmem-4 is best suited for small databases, caching layers, and legacy enterprise applications requiring moderate memory capacity without high compute demands.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 26 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-heavy workloads requiring moderate compute performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-4 features 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 26 GB of RAM, offering a memory-optimized profile for moderate workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads with moderate processing requirements.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 26.0 GB of RAM (6.5 GB per core)
Powered by an Intel Xeon 2.3 GHz processor (x86_64)
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Moderate memory bandwidth of 25.86 GB/sec for large blocks
Best suited for caching layers, small databases, and memory-heavy legacy apps
